channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Artemis II astronauts set to break Apollo 13's distance record with moon flyby
item.description: The Artemis II mission is set to break the distance record previously held by the Apollo 13 astronauts. Four crew members—Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch from NASA, and Jeremy Hansen from ...

item.title: Former Nova Scotia Correctional Officer Sentenced for Smuggling Cannabis into Jail
item.description: A recent sentencing hearing in Nova Scotia has brought attention to the issue of contraband and drug trafficking within correctional facilities. Robert John McLaughlin, 42, a former correctional offic...

item.title: Kenyan MPs Discuss SEZ Amendment to Boost Local Oil and Gas Investment
item.description: Lawmakers in Kenya have met with Gulf Energy to deliberate proposed amendments to the Special Economic Zones (SEZ) Act. The changes aim to expand tax concessions and incentives to include upstream and...

item.title: Ombudsman preparing plunder case over alleged flood control anomalies involving Romualdez and Escudero
item.description: The Office of the Ombudsman is preparing to file plunder charges against former Speaker Martin Romualdez and former Senate President Francis 'Chiz' Escudero in connection with alleged irregularities i...

item.title: Scholarship Opportunities: UK Postgraduation for Indian Students
item.description: So, you're an Indian student wanting to do your postgraduation in the UK, eh? Good on ya! Looks like there are some solid scholarships available. This piece lists twelve different options, covering a ...

item.title: IFP Urges South Africans to Ensure Safety and Peace During Easter Weekend
item.description: The Inkatha Freedom Party (IFP) has wished South Africans a blessed and safe Easter weekend. The party’s president, Hon. Velenkosini Hlabisa, called on all road users, both motorists and pedestrians, ...

item.title: DSWD to begin cash assistance distribution for tricycle drivers outside Metro Manila on April 8
item.description: The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) is set to begin distributing ₱5,000 cash assistance to tricycle drivers outside Metro Manila starting on April 8, 2026. This initiative, under t...

item.title: Artemis 2 Astronaut Victor Glover Shares Reflective Easter Message During Lunar Mission
item.description: NASA astronaut Victor Glover, currently serving as pilot on the Artemis 2 mission, delivered an unplanned Easter reflection from space as the Orion spacecraft approached the moon on April 4, 2026. His...

item.title: DR Congo to Receive US Deportees Under Agreement with Trump Administration
item.description: Rais Donald Trump's serikali imefanya makubaliano na Jamhuri ya Kidemokrasia ya Kongo (DRC) ambapo nchi itapokea watu waliotolewa Marekani, kulingana na taarifa kutoka Wizara ya Mawasiliano ya Kongo. ...

item.title: Cameroon President Paul Biya Names Son Franck Emmanuel Biya as Vice President and Military Chief
item.description: For Cameroon, yawa don gas. President Paul Biya don appoint him son, Franck Emmanuel Biya, as Vice President and head of the armed forces. Dis move dey strengthen the President power inside both polit...

item.title: Key national and international updates: Delhi Assembly security breach, killing of IRGC intel chief, LPG policy change, and more
item.description: In a series of major updates today, security was briefly compromised at the Delhi Assembly as a Tata Sierra with Uttar Pradesh plates broke through the barricades and entered the Assembly complex thro...

item.title: Viral video wrongly claims Iranian forces captured US pilot; original footage from Libya
item.description: Recently, a video went viral on multiple social media platforms claiming it showed Iranian soldiers capturing a US pilot after downing an F-15 fighter jet. However, fact-checking revealed this video i...

item.title: Senate rejects PTI proposal to expand women’s role in politics
item.description: A Senate committee in Pakistan has turned down a bill that sought to strengthen women’s role in the political process. The bill, introduced by PTI Senator Dr Zarqa Suharwardy Taimur, proposed amendmen...

item.title: PM Shehbaz promotes adoption of electric vehicles to ease fuel burden and support economic stability
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, speaking at a ceremony in Islamabad on Monday, stressed the importance of adopting electric vehicles (EVs) across Pakistan as a step towards reducing fuel dependency and...

item.title: why women investors are rising in Kenya's stock market
item.description: The Nation article examines the rising participation of women in Kenya’s stock market, highlighting factors such as financial literacy initiatives, regulatory changes, and targeted investment products...

item.title: The Rise of Sauna Culture in the UK: A Social and Health Perspective
item.description: Sauna culture is rapidly expanding across the UK, including Wales, with more than 600 public saunas now available. Advocates suggest these heated spaces could serve as social hubs similar to tradition...

item.title: Police in Brantford Respond to Fatal Shooting Incident, Suspect Arrested
item.description: Brantford Police are investigating a fatal shooting that occurred in the city, resulting in the death of an individual. Authorities confirmed that a suspect has been taken into custody, though details...

item.title: Pakistan's Austerity Measures: Cosmetic Steps or Real Reform?
item.description: On March 20, 2026, Pakistan’s Prime Minister made a public address highlighting his refusal of a proposed oil price hike, following earlier mild austerity measures. Critics argue that such measures, i...

item.title: DP Kindiki Urges All Eligible Kenyans to Register to Vote Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Deputy President Kithure Kindiki has urged all eligible Kenyans who have not yet registered to vote to do so before the registration period closes. Speaking in Irunduni, Tharaka Nithi County, during E...

item.title: Rio Tinto’s Matt Holcz Faces a Long Road in Developing $20 Billion Mines
item.description: Matt Holcz, Rio Tinto's iron ore boss, has been a key figure in the company’s operations in Western Australia’s Pilbara region. Known for his long-standing connection to the area, Holcz is facing a mo...
